questions.text: Work done in moving a charge of 6C between two points is10J. What is the potential difference between two points?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula : V = W/Q
Incorrect as per formula : V = W/Q
Incorrect as per formula : V = W/Q

The formula is: V = W/QGiven that the work done ( W ) is 10J and the charge ( Q ) is 6C, the potential difference ( V ) can be calculated as: V = 10J/6C V = 5J/3C V = 1.66 VSo, the potential difference between the two points is 1.66 volts.

questions.explanation: The formula is: V = W/QGiven that the work done ( W ) is 10J and the charge ( Q ) is 6C, the potential difference ( V ) can be calculated as: V = 10J/6C V = 5J/3C V = 1.66 VSo, the potential difference between the two points is 1.66 volts.

questions.text: Four charges +Q,-Q,+Q,-Q are placed at the corners of a square taken in order. At the centre of the square.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: E=0: This would mean that the electric field at the center of the square is zero. This is true because the electric field vectors due to each charge cancel each other out due to symmetry.V=0: On the other hand, it is a scalar quantity, which means it only has magnitude and no direction. The potential due to each of the charges at the center of the square will add up algebraically. Since the charges are equal and opposite, their potentials do not cancel out, resulting in a non-zero electric potential at the center.

E=0: As explained above, the electric field is zero at the center due to the cancellation of the field vectors.V≠0: The electric potential is not zero because the potentials from individual charges add up, resulting in a non-zero net potential at the center.

E≠0: This would suggest that the electric field at the center is not zero, which is incorrect for the given configuration of charges.V=0: This implies that the electric potential is zero, which, is previously explained.

E≠0: This option also incorrect as it suggests a non-zero electric field at the center.V≠0: It states that the electric potential is not zero, the presence of a non-zero electric field makes this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: In summary, the correct answer is E=0, V≠0, because at the center of the square, the electric field vectors cancel out due to symmetry, resulting in zero electric field, while the potentials from the charges add up to give a non-zero electric potential.
hints: at the corners of a square taken in order

questions.text: A wire of resistance ‘R' is stretched till its radius is half of the original value. The 2R resistance of stretched wire is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect according to formula: R = ρ L/A
Incorrect according to formula: R = ρ L/A
Incorrect according to formula: R = ρ L/A

The resistance (R) of a wire is given by formula: R = ρ L/Awhere ( ρ ) is the resistivity of the material, ( L ) is the length of the wire, and ( A ) is the cross-sectional area.When the wire is stretched and its radius is halved, its cross-sectional area ( A ) becomes one-fourth, since ( A ) is proportional to the square of the radius (( r2 )). However, the volume (and thus the mass) of the wire remains constant, so the length ( L ) of the wire must increase to compensate for the reduced cross-sectional area.The new resistance ( R’ ) of the wire can be found by substituting the new length ( L’ ) and new area ( A’ ) into the resistance formula: R' = ρ L'/A'Given that ( L’ ) is the new length and ( A’ ) is the new area, which is one-fourth of the original area, we can express ( L’ ) in terms of the original length ( L ) due to the conservation of volume: L′= 4LSubstituting ( L’ ) and ( A’ ) into the formula for ( R’ ), we get: R′ = 4L/(1/4)A ​ =16ρL/A ​=16RTherefore, the new resistance of the wire when its radius is halved is 16R.

questions.explanation: The resistance (R) of a wire is given by formula: R = ρ L/Awhere ( ρ ) is the resistivity of the material, ( L ) is the length of the wire, and ( A ) is the cross-sectional area.When the wire is stretched and its radius is halved, its cross-sectional area ( A ) becomes one-fourth, since ( A ) is proportional to the square of the radius (( r2 )). However, the volume (and thus the mass) of the wire remains constant, so the length ( L ) of the wire must increase to compensate for the reduced cross-sectional area.The new resistance ( R’ ) of the wire can be found by substituting the new length ( L’ ) and new area ( A’ ) into the resistance formula: R' = ρ L'/A'Given that ( L’ ) is the new length and ( A’ ) is the new area, which is one-fourth of the original area, we can express ( L’ ) in terms of the original length ( L ) due to the conservation of volume: L′= 4LSubstituting ( L’ ) and ( A’ ) into the formula for ( R’ ), we get: R′ = 4L/(1/4)A ​ =16ρL/A ​=16RTherefore, the new resistance of the wire when its radius is halved is 16R.
hints: radius is half of the original value

questions.text: Energy consumed by a 60-watt bulb in 2 minutes:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: P = 60W t = 2 minutes =2×60 seconds =120 seconds E= P×twhere ( E ) is the energy in joules (J), ( P ) is the power in watts (W), and ( t ) is the time in seconds (s). E=60 W×120 s E=7200 JSo, the energy consumed by a 60-watt bulb in 2 minutes is 7200 joules. As we want the answer in kilojoules (kJ), so we'll divide the energy in joules by 1000 because 1 kJ = 1000 J: E=7200/1000 J​ E=7.2 kJTherefore, the energy consumed is 7.2 kilojoules.
Incorrect as per formula: E= P×t
Incorrect as per formula: E= P×t
Incorrect as per formula: E= P×t

questions.explanation: P = 60W t = 2 minutes =2×60 seconds =120 seconds E= P×twhere ( E ) is the energy in joules (J), ( P ) is the power in watts (W), and ( t ) is the time in seconds (s). E=60 W×120 s E=7200 JSo, the energy consumed by a 60-watt bulb in 2 minutes is 7200 joules. As we want the answer in kilojoules (kJ), so we'll divide the energy in joules by 1000 because 1 kJ = 1000 J: E=7200/1000 J​ E=7.2 kJTherefore, the energy consumed is 7.2 kilojoules.

questions.text: The unit of magnetic flux is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The unit Wb.m-2 is the unit of magnetic flux density, which is equivalent to one tesla (T).

The unit Wb.m2 is not a standard unit in the International System of Units (SI). However, if we consider the weber (Wb) which is the SI unit of magnetic flux, and square meters (m^2) which is a unit of area, it seems like it is referring to a quantity related to magnetic flux over an area.
The unit of magnetic flux is the weber (Wb).
The tesla (T) is the unit of magnetic flux density.

questions.explanation: The unit of magnetic flux is the weber (Wb). In the CGS system, it is measured in maxwells.

questions.text: The working principle of galvanometer is based upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is not a perfect quantity that can be base of principle of galvanometer.

The working principle of a galvanometer is indeed based on the torque that acts on a current-carrying coil placed within a magnetic field. This torque causes the coil to rotate, and the angle of rotation is proportional to the current flowing through the coil, allowing for its measurement.
It is not a perfect quantity that can be base of principle of galvanometer.
It is not a perfect quantity that can be base of principle of galvanometer.

questions.explanation: The working principle of a galvanometer is indeed based on the torque that acts on a current-carrying coil placed within a magnetic field. This torque causes the coil to rotate, and the angle of rotation is proportional to the current flowing through the coil, allowing for its measurement.

questions.text: If the motor is overloaded then the magnitude of back e.m.f:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect with respect to formula: V = Eb​ + Ia​Ra

When a motor is overloaded, its speed decreases due to the increased load. As a result, the magnitude of the back electromotive force (e.m.f.) also decreases. This is because back e.m.f is proportional to the motor’s speed. As V = Eb​ + Ia​Ra
Incorrect with respect to formula: V = Eb​ + Ia​Ra
Incorrect with respect to formula: V = Eb​ + Ia​Ra​

questions.text: The current in a coil of inductance 5H decreases at the rate of 2A/s. The induced e.m.f is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula: ε=−L ΔI/Δt​
Incorrect as per formula: ε=−L ΔI/Δt​
Incorrect as per formula: ε=−L ΔI/Δt​

To find the induced electromotive force (e.m.f) in a coil when the current is changing, you can use the formula: ε=−L ΔI/Δt​ Given that the inductance ( L ) is 5H and the rate of change of current ( ΔI/Δt​ ) is 2A/s, we can substitute these values into the formula: ε=−5×2 ε=−10 VSo, the induced e.m.f is -10 volts. The negative sign indicates that the induced e.m.f is in a direction that opposes the change in current, according to Lenz’s Law.

questions.explanation: To find the induced electromotive force (e.m.f) in a coil when the current is changing, you can use the formula: ε=−L ΔI/Δt​ Given that the inductance ( L ) is 5H and the rate of change of current ( ΔI/Δt​ ) is 2A/s, we can substitute these values into the formula: ε=−5×2 ε=−10 VSo, the induced e.m.f is -10 volts. The negative sign indicates that the induced e.m.f is in a direction that opposes the change in current, according to Lenz’s Law.
hints: urrent in a coil of inductance 5H decreases at the rate of 2A/s

questions.text: The phase difference between the current and voltage at resonance in R.L.C series A.C circuit is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: At resonance in an RLC series AC circuit, the inductive reactance (XL) and capacitive reactance (XC) are equal and cancel each other out. This means that the circuit behaves as if it were purely resistive, and the current and voltage are in phase. Therefore, the phase difference between the current and voltage at resonance is: ϕ=0∘ or 0 radiansThis indicates that there is no phase difference; the current and voltage rise and fall together.
It is incorrect because there is no phase difference; the current and voltage rise and fall together.
It is incorrect because there is no phase difference; the current and voltage rise and fall together.
It is incorrect because there is no phase difference; the current and voltage rise and fall together.

questions.explanation: At resonance in an RLC series AC circuit, the inductive reactance (XL) and capacitive reactance (XC) are equal and cancel each other out. This means that the circuit behaves as if it were purely resistive, and the current and voltage are in phase. Therefore, the phase difference between the current and voltage at resonance is: ϕ=0∘ or 0 radiansThis indicates that there is no phase difference; the current and voltage rise and fall together.
hints: phase difference between the current and voltage

questions.text: A body which breaks down just after crossing the elastic limit is known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is material’s ability to return to its original shape and size after the forces causing deformation are removed.
It is measure of a material’s resistance to localized plastic deformation, such as scratching etc.
It is material’s capacity to undergo significant plastic deformation under tensile stress before rupture.
A body that breaks down immediately after crossing the elastic limit is known as brittle.

questions.explanation: A body that breaks down immediately after crossing the elastic limit is known as brittle.
hints: breaks down just after crossing the elastic limit

questions.text: Which factor does not affect the conductivity of a PN junction diode?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The process of adding impurities to the semiconductor material (doping) greatly affects the conductivity of the diode. N-type doping adds more electrons, while P-type doping adds more holes, both of which increase conductivity.
As temperature increases, the number of charge carriers (electrons and holes) also increases, which can lead to higher conductivity.
Applying an external voltage to a PN junction diode affects its conductivity by allowing current to flow more easily in one direction than the other, depending on the polarity of the voltage.

Pressure does not directly affect the conductivity of a PN junction diode. The conductivity is primarily dependent on the electronic properties of the semiconductor, such as the concentration of charge carriers and the energy gap, which are not significantly altered by pressure.

questions.explanation: Pressure is the factor that does not affect the conductivity of a PN junction diode. while the other factors; voltage, temperature, and doping are all factors that can influence the diode’s conductivity.
hints: conductivity of a PN junction diode

questions.text: Photon A has twice the energy of photon B. What is the ratio of the momentum of A to that of B?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is incorrect with respect to formula; P = E.c

The momentum ( P ) of a photon is directly proportional to its energy ( E ), as given by the equation P = E.c where ( c ) is the speed of light.Since Photon A has twice the energy of Photon B ( EA = 2EB ), their momentum will also have the same ratio:Therefore, the ratio of the momentum of Photon A to that of Photon B is ( PA : PB = 2:1 ).
It is incorrect with respect to formula; P = E.c
It is incorrect with respect to formula; P = E.c

questions.explanation: The momentum ( P ) of a photon is directly proportional to its energy ( E ), as given by the equation P = E.c where ( c ) is the speed of light.Since Photon A has twice the energy of Photon B ( EA = 2EB ), their momentum will also have the same ratio:Therefore, the ratio of the momentum of Photon A to that of Photon B is ( PA : PB = 2:1 ).
hints: ratio of the momentum of A to that of B

questions.text: Pair Production occurs only when energy of photon is at least equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is incorrect because it's 1.02keV not 1.02MeV.
It is incorrect because it's just 1.02eV not 1.02MeV.
Pair production occurs when the energy of a photon is at least equal to the combined rest mass energy of the electron-positron pair that is produced. This minimum energy threshold is 1.02 MeV.
It is incorrect because it's 1.02GeV not 1.02MeV.

questions.explanation: Pair production occurs when the energy of a photon is at least equal to the combined rest mass energy of the electron-positron pair that is produced. This minimum energy threshold is 1.02 MeV. The other are wrong because they have different units.
hints: energy of photon is at least equal to

questions.text: Which of the following series of hydrogen spectra lies in the ultraviolet region of the spectrum?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The series of the hydrogen spectrum that lies in the ultraviolet region is the Lyman series. This series is characterized by the electron transitions from higher energy levels ( n = 2,3,4.... ) to the lowest energy level ( n = 1 ).
This series of hydrogen spectrum series lies in visible region.
This series of hydrogen spectrum series lies in infrared region.
This series of hydrogen spectrum series lies in far infrared region.

questions.explanation: The series of the hydrogen spectrum that lies in the ultraviolet region is the Lyman series. This series is characterized by the electron transitions from higher energy levels ( n = 2,3,4.... ) to the lowest energy level ( n = 1 ).
hints: series of hydrogen spectra lies in the ultraviolet region

questions.text: lf an electron jumps from the second orbit to first orbit in hydrogen atom, it emits photon of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is wrong with respect to formula;Ephoton​=E1−E2

When an electron in a hydrogen atom transitions from the second orbit (n=2) to the first orbit (n=1), it emits a photon with a specific energy. This energy corresponds to the difference in energy levels between these two orbits. According to the Bohr model, the energy ( E ) of an electron in a particular orbit ( n ) is given by: E(n)=−13.6 eV /n2​The energy of the photon emitted during the transition is the difference in energy between the two levels: Ephoton​=E1−E2 = -Eo/n2 =−13.6 eV(1/12​−1/22​) Ephoton​=13.6 eV(1/4 - 1​) =13.6 eV×3/4​ =10.20 eVSo, the photon emitted has an energy of 10.20 eV. This is the characteristic energy of the photon emitted in the Lyman series of the hydrogen spectrum when an electron falls from the second to the first orbit.
This is wrong with respect to formula;Ephoton​=E1−E2
This is wrong with respect to formula;Ephoton​=E1−E2

questions.explanation: When an electron in a hydrogen atom transitions from the second orbit (n=2) to the first orbit (n=1), it emits a photon with a specific energy. This energy corresponds to the difference in energy levels between these two orbits. According to the Bohr model, the energy ( E ) of an electron in a particular orbit ( n ) is given by: E(n)=−13.6 eV /n2​The energy of the photon emitted during the transition is the difference in energy between the two levels: Ephoton​=E1−E2 = -Eo/n2 =−13.6 eV(1/12​−1/22​) Ephoton​=13.6 eV(1/4 - 1​) =13.6 eV×3/4​ =10.20 eVSo, the photon emitted has an energy of 10.20 eV. This is the characteristic energy of the photon emitted in the Lyman series of the hydrogen spectrum when an electron falls from the second to the first orbit.
hints: from the second orbit to first orbit

questions.text: The quantity of uranium is 400g, the amount of uranium left after three half-lives is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect as per formula; Final amount=Initial amount×(1/2)number of half-lives
This is incorrect as per formula; Final amount=Initial amount×(1/2)number of half-lives

To calculate the amount of uranium left after three half-lives, you can use the formula:Final amount=Initial amount×(1/2​)number of half-livesGiven the initial amount of uranium is 400g, after three half-lives, the calculation would be:Final amount=400g×(1/2​)3 =400g×1/8​ =50gSo, after three half-lives, 50 grams of uranium would remain.
This is incorrect as per formula; Final amount=Initial amount×(1/2)number of half-lives

questions.explanation: To calculate the amount of uranium left after three half-lives, you can use the formula:Final amount=Initial amount×(1/2​)number of half-livesGiven the initial amount of uranium is 400g, after three half-lives, the calculation would be:Final amount=400g×(1/2​)3 =400g×1/8​ =50gSo, after three half-lives, 50 grams of uranium would remain.

questions.text: The particles that are experiencing strong nuclear force are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Photons are the quantum of the electromagnetic field and are responsible for electromagnetic force. They do not interact with the strong nuclear force but are crucial for the interactions between charged particles.
Leptons are a family of particles that do not experience the strong nuclear force.
Bosons are a family of particles that do not experience the strong nuclear force

The particles that experience the strong nuclear force are hadrons. The strong nuclear force is a short-range but extremely powerful force that acts between these particles, holding the nucleus of an atom together.

questions.explanation: The particles that experience the strong nuclear force are hadrons. The strong nuclear force is a short-range but extremely powerful force that acts between these particles, holding the nucleus of an atom together.
